mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and efficient model serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations to maximize performance.

steps.title: Add the vLLM Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.0

```

> This adds the vLLM repository for version 0.19.0.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for vLLM to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.19.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> Ensure CRDs are installed before deploying the application.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.19.0

```

> Replace 'vllm' with your desired release name.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to deploy a model with vLLM.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

```

> Modify the model name as per your requirements.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM deployment and ensures that the Custom Resource is ready.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Look for the 'READY' status in the output.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command removes the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM to a new version
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary data. This process involves draining the pods, upgrading, and verifying the deployment.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of your node.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> This command allows scheduling on the node again.
> Verify that the vLLM instance is running and ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the vLLM Helm chart is installed and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition is already installed in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Skip the CRD installation step if it already exists.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the model configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for vLLM.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust resource requests in the deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME              READY   STATUS    AGE' with your vLLM instance listed and its status as 'True'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
